# Running with ./... catches template errors in deeply nested packages.

! check-templates ./...
stderr 'article\.html.*Missing not found on example\.com/app/sub/deep/nested\.Article \(E001\)'

-- go.mod --
module example.com/app

go 1.25.0
-- main.go --
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
	fmt.Println("root")
}
-- sub/handler.go --
package sub

import (
	"embed"
	"fmt"
	"html/template"
	"net/http"
)

var (
	//go:embed *.html
	source embed.FS

	templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(source, "*"))
)

type Page struct {
	Title string
}

func Handle(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	_ = templates.ExecuteTemplate(w, "page.html", Page{Title: "Hello"})
}

var _ = fmt.Sprint

-- sub/page.html --
<html>
<head><title>{{.Title}}</title></head>
</html>
-- sub/deep/nested/handler.go --
package nested

import (
	"embed"
	"fmt"
	"html/template"
	"net/http"
)

var (
	//go:embed *.html
	viewFS embed.FS

	views = template.Must(template.ParseFS(viewFS, "*"))
)

type Article struct {
	Headline string
}

func ServeArticle(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	_ = views.ExecuteTemplate(w, "article.html", Article{Headline: "Test"})
}

var _ = fmt.Sprint

-- sub/deep/nested/article.html --
<article>
<h1>{{.Headline}}</h1>
<p>{{.Missing}}</p>
</article>
